title: Continuity integrity validation is enforced by a dedicated repository workflow
summary: A dedicated GitHub Actions workflow now runs the continuity test suite and the aggregate continuity-record validator whenever continuity records, continuity tooling, package scripts, or the workflow itself changes. The gate is read-only, bounded to five minutes, and uses concurrency cancellation to prevent redundant stale runs. This closes the previously recorded gap between having a validator and enforcing it at repository boundaries.

status_relations.observed: The repository contains continuity validation code, adversarial tests, package-level test and verification commands, and a path-scoped GitHub Actions workflow.
status_relations.inferred: A pull request that introduces duplicate IDs, dangling relations, invalid state values, or malformed continuity JSON should fail the dedicated job before merge when branch protection requires the check.
status_relations.proposed: Make the continuity-integrity job a required branch-protection check after its first successful run is observed.
status_relations.superseded: The unresolved CI-wiring statement inside CM-0011 is historically preserved but no longer describes current repository source state.
status_relations.unresolved: No completed GitHub Actions run for commit 34fb76635c8066b96b4606fa1cd5e2c60a63468b was observable during this run, and branch protection enforcement was not verified.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch package.json and .github/workflows/continuity-integrity.yml at or after commit 34fb76635c8066b96b4606fa1cd5e2c60a63468b. Reject this record if npm run verify:continuity does not execute both tests and aggregate validation, if the workflow is absent or not path-scoped to continuity changes, or if an invalid fixture can pass the workflow.
